mission.title: argo: Explicitly document the Kubernetes version compatibility for each ArgoCD release in a single location
mission.description: Explicitly document the Kubernetes version compatibility for each ArgoCD release in a single location. This issue affects 74+ users.

steps.title: Identify argo feature symptoms
steps.description:
Check for the issue in your argo deployment:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n argo -l app=argo
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=argo -n argo --tail=100 | grep -i error
```
Look for errors related to: Explicitly document the Kubernetes version compatibility for each ArgoCD release in a single location

steps.title: Check current argo configuration
steps.description:
Review the relevant argo configuration:
In a single table in the documentation, create a table which states for every ArgoCD minor version which kubernetes versions it is compatible with.

steps.title: Apply the fix for Explicitly document the Kubernetes version compatibility
steps.description:
Closes https://github.com/argoproj/argo-cd/issues/12276

Demo: https://github.com/crenshaw-dev/argo-cd/pull/19/files#diff-4ab2c3ee7a77d52cc08fc2cc29333bb3b970adb5cc69bbd185a3b596c5305ad6

See the fix PR for details: linked PR

steps.title: Confirm Explicitly document the Kubernetes version is resolved
steps.description:
Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=argo -n argo --tail=50 --since=5m
kubectl get events -n argo --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm that the issue symptoms are gone.

resolution.summary:
The root cause is: Closes https://github.com/argoproj/argo-cd/issues/12276

Demo: https://github.com/crenshaw-dev/argo-cd/pull/19/files#diff-4ab2c3ee7a77d52cc08fc2cc29333bb3b970adb5cc69bbd185a3b596c5305ad6.

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with argo installed or the issue environment reproducible.
